req.title: System-Admin views benefit
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to view a benefit through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-036 mandates that an authenticated system-admin can view a benefit via GET /api/benefits, receiving HTTP 200/201 with an entity id, and that an audit record is written on success. Error paths require 400 for validation failures, 401 for unauthenticated requests, and 403 for forbidden roles. Five tests are designed: (1) EP positive – valid admin views an existing benefit; (2) NEG – unauthenticated request returns 401; (3) NEG/DT – authenticated non-admin role returns 403; (4) BVA – request with a missing mandatory field (benefit id = empty string) returns 400; (5) STC – audit record exists after a successful view, confirming state transition from 'viewed' to 'audited'.

tests.name: test_admin_views_benefit_returns_200_with_entity_id
actions: Obtain a valid admin auth token via <get_admin_token>
expected: Response body contains 'id' field equal to <KNOWN_BENEFIT_ID>
Response body contains all mandatory benefit fields
trace: R-WEB-036: Expected outcomes – benefit is viewed; response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app/api"
KNOWN_BENEFIT_ID = "<KNOWN_BENEFIT_ID>"

@pytest.fixture
def admin_headers():
    token = <get_admin_token>()
    return {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}

def test_admin_views_benefit_returns_200_with_entity_id(admin_headers):
    resp = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/benefits/{KNOWN_BENEFIT_ID}", headers=admin_headers)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("id") == KNOWN_BENEFIT_ID
    assert "name" in body

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_request_returns_401
actions: Send GET /api/benefits/<KNOWN_BENEFIT_ID> without any Authorization header
expected: Response body contains an error or message field indicating authentication is required
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app/api"
KNOWN_BENEFIT_ID = "<KNOWN_BENEFIT_ID>"

def test_unauthenticated_request_returns_401():
    resp = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/benefits/{KNOWN_BENEFIT_ID}")
    assert resp.status_code == 401
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_returns_403
preconditions: Caller is authenticated but has role 'employee' (not system-admin)
actions: Obtain a valid non-admin auth token via <get_employee_token>
Send GET /api/benefits/<KNOWN_BENEFIT_ID> with the employee Authorization header
expected: Response body contains an error or message field indicating access is forbidden
trace: R-WEB-036: Error pathways – 403 if role forbidden

tests.name: test_missing_mandatory_benefit_id_returns_400
actions: Obtain a valid admin auth token via <get_admin_token>
Send GET /api/benefits/ with an empty benefit id path segment (boundary: id length = 0)
expected: Response body contains validation error details referencing the missing or invalid id
trace: R-WEB-036: Error pathways – 400 on validation error
R-WEB-036: Statement – mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app/api"

@pytest.fixture
def admin_headers():
    token = <get_admin_token>()
    return {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}

def test_missing_mandatory_benefit_id_returns_400(admin_headers):
    # Boundary: benefit id is an empty string (length 0)
    resp = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/benefits/", headers=admin_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 400
    body = resp.json()
    error_text = str(body)
    assert "id" in error_text.lower() or "invalid" in error_text.lower() or "required" in error_text.lower()

tests.name: test_audit_record_written_after_successful_view
actions: Obtain a valid admin auth token via <get_admin_token>
Retrieve the latest audit record for this benefit via <get_latest_audit_record>
expected: Audit record exists referencing entity id <KNOWN_BENEFIT_ID>
Audit record action field indicates a view/read event
trace: R-WEB-036: Expected outcomes – audit record written on success
R-WEB-036: State transition – benefit viewed → audit record created
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app/api"
KNOWN_BENEFIT_ID = "<KNOWN_BENEFIT_ID>"

@pytest.fixture
def admin_headers():
    token = <get_admin_token>()
    return {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}

def test_audit_record_written_after_successful_view(admin_headers):
    resp = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/benefits/{KNOWN_BENEFIT_ID}", headers=admin_headers)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    audit = <get_latest_audit_record>(entity_id=KNOWN_BENEFIT_ID, action="view")
    assert audit is not None
    assert audit.get("entity_id") == KNOWN_BENEFIT_ID
    assert audit.get("action", "").lower() in ("view", "read", "get")
